QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_1

question.text: What is the minimum net worth requirement for a Category I Merchant Banker under SEBI regulations?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 7, Category I merchant bankers must maintain a net worth of at least Rs. 50 crore. This higher threshold reflects their ability to carry out ALL permitted merchant banking activities including main board public equity issues. **The Trap:** Rs. 10 crore is tempting because it is the actual requirement for Category II — candidates who confuse the two categories will pick this. **Cross-Exam Context:** Net worth requirements for intermediaries are also tested in NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors) and Series VIII (Equity Derivatives). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always verify an MB's category before engaging them for a main board IPO — only Category I can handle it.
